rationale: The cited paper appears only once in the Discussion as part of a grouped citation suggesting a possible moral-licensing explanation for mixed purchase shifts. The citing study does not build hypotheses from Khan and Dhar (2006), operationalize its constructs, or test a licensing mechanism directly. Its core design and identification strategy focus on campaign effects in transaction data and survey awareness outcomes, independent of the cited work. Thus the citation functions as a brief interpretive background mention rather than a substantive dependency.
